---
title: "LRP65: Adjusted model — independent baseline predictors of word-reading gain"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP65 is the Bayesian "interpretable estimand" follow-up to the LightGBM discovery
layer (LRP01). It answers the **static, between-child** question raised by the
descriptive work and the meeting: *do children who start (at wave 1) with more
letter-sound knowledge / language / blending go on to gain more in word reading?*

The candidate predictors form a correlated general-ability cluster (vocabulary–block
design $\rho \approx 0.63$; vocabulary–letter-sounds $\approx 0.4$–$0.5$), so a naive
"throw them all in" regression is multicollinear and "independent predictor" is
ambiguous. The DAG below fixes the estimand and the adjustment set **before** fitting.

## Estimand and design

::: {.callout-important title="Between-child association — match the design to the estimand"}

The estimand is the **mutually-adjusted between-child association** of each wave-1
baseline skill with subsequent word-reading gain, holding the other measured baselines
and $W_{\text{pre}}$ constant, *across children*.

Design: **one row per child** — $x$ = each child's wave-1 (T1) baseline level, $y$ =
word reading at the last wave conditioned on $W_{\text{T1}}$ (the gain framing). There
is deliberately **no** child random intercept: with one row per child the coefficients
are genuinely between-child. A pooled all-phase design with a child random intercept
would instead estimate the *within-child* association — a different question (handled by
the deferred cross-lagged model), so it is **not** used here. The trade-off is explicit:
dropping the intercept also **forgoes the partial latent-ability repair** it provides in
the `gain_factors`/`level_factors` and DiD families (revised DAG ID-2; DiD design note
Decision 4). With general ability `GA` latent, this pure between-child contrast is fully
exposed to time-invariant ability confounding with **no repair available** — so the
no-intercept design is the cleaner *match to the between-child estimand*, not a cleaner
causal contrast. (This is also why it stays a standalone `lrp-rli-adj-065` rather than folding into
the `lrpgf` family: those pool the three period transitions with a child intercept — a
within+between blend — whereas this is the one-row-per-child between-child estimand.)

These are **associations, not causal effects** (n ≈ 51). The randomised word-reading ITT
(now `lrp-rli-itt-010`, which supersedes LRP52; the randomised gain-side coefficient lives in
`lrp-rli-gf-001`) carries the causal claim about the programme.

:::

![Causal DAG. Latent general ability `g` (dashed, not fitted) drives the correlated T1 baselines; the dashed edges from non-verbal MA, behaviour and SES to gain are the associations the model is built to test.](dag.svg){#fig-dag .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Adjustment set and predictors

- **Predictors of interest (standardised T1, per-SD):** letter sounds (`yarclet`), an
  equal-weight **language composite** (ROWPVT + EOWPVT + CELF), blending, age.
- **Baseline conditioning:** $W_{\text{pre}}$ (T1 word reading) via $\gamma_{\text{own}}$.
- **Tested covariates:** non-verbal MA (block design) and behaviour, entered to
  *demonstrate* whether they carry independent signal. SES is adjudicated in a separate
  complete-case sensitivity fit (it has notable missingness).

Each predictor's **adjusted** (mutual) coefficient is reported alongside its **bivariate**
(baseline-only-adjusted) association, so the shared-variance shift is visible.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_adjusted.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
